steps.title: Read the intercepts
steps.content: From the graph, the line crosses the axes at $(0,6)$ and $(2,0)$. Use these two points to find the equation.

steps.title: Write the line equation
steps.content:
Find the slope and write the equation in slope-intercept form.
$$m=\frac{0-6}{2-0}=-3$$
$$y=-3x+6$$

steps.title: Match the required form
steps.content:
Rewrite the equation so the constant on the right is $36$.
$$y=-3x+6$$
$$3x+y=6$$
$$18x+6y=36$$
This matches $18x+Ry=36$ with positive $R=6$. The answer is C.
